message.content:
Sure, here is the alignment of the lines of code with the corresponding parts of the transcript:

```json
[
    {"text": "everyone welcome back and let's write", "code": ["# Definition for a binary tree node.", "# class TreeNode:", "#     def __init__(self, val=0, left=None, right=None):", "#         self.val = val", "#         self.left = left", "#         self.right = right"]}, 

    {"text": "today and this problem actually has", "code": ["# RECURSIVE DFS", "class Solution:", "    def maxDepth(self, root: Optional[TreeNode]) -> int:"]},

    {"text": "what's the base case we're using", "code": ["        if not root:", "            return 0"]},

    {"text": "so the maximum of the left and right", "code": ["        return 1 + max(self.maxDepth(root.left), self.maxDepth(root.right))"]},

    {"text": "we're counting the number of nodes so in", "code": ["# ITERATIVE DFS", "class Solution:", "    def maxDepth(self, root: Optional[TreeNode]) -> int:", "        stack = [[root, 1]]", "        res = 0", "        while stack:", "            node, depth = stack.pop()", "            if node:", "                res = max(res, depth)", "                stack.append([node.left, depth + 1])", "                stack.append([node.right, depth + 1])", "        return res"]}, 

    {"text": "maintain the depth so we're", "code": ["# BFS", "class Solution:", "    def maxDepth(self, root: Optional[TreeNode]) -> int:", "        q = deque()", "        if root:", "            q.append(root)", "        level = 0", "        while q:", "            for i in range(len(q)):"]},

    {"text": "adding nodes to the stack but we're also", "code": ["                node = q.popleft()", "                if node.left:", "                    q.append(node.left)", "                if node.right:", "                    q.append(node.right)", "            level += 1", "        return level"]}
]
```

This JSON format maps specific pieces of the transcript to the related pieces of code. The aim is to provide a direct linkage between the spoken parts of the solution and the respective code implementation for better understanding and learning.
